title: End-to-End Release, Incident Recovery, and Engineering Defense · Runtime Contract: Pokedex Go Migration Rollback
summary: A candidate passes unit tests but lacks clean-build, race, accessibility, fair-use, failure, migration, rollback, restore, and stakeholder evidence, while residual risks have no owners. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go migration rollback increment in defensible recoverable Go Pokedex release dossier. Learners progress through…
objectives: Prove forward migration, old-binary compatibility limits, backup, rollback, restored collection integrity, and support decision points.
Rehearse invalid input, API not found, timeout, malformed response, cancellation, cache corruption, write interruption, disk failure, and signal shutdown.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Predict Pokedex Go Migration Rollback · Theory Pokedex Go Migrat…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 5974 during an accessible regional field-guide session. Build deterministic Go evidence to defend reconciled release, migration, rollback, restore, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; all goroutines, bodies, signals, timers, and files require named cleanup ownership; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
